ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for loaded fries with chicken and sriracha sauce as a composite dish. FOODON_00002490 (french fries food product) used as closest parent for the fries component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (chicken meat, cooked) used as closest parent for the dominant protein component. No composite fast-food loaded fries code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this composite fast-food dish.

serving.description: 1 meal portion (loaded fries with chicken and sriracha sauce)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal serving of chicken loaded fries with sriracha sauce, approximately 350 g, as served at Jollibee Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Fast foods, french fries, with chicken and sauce, composite estimate based on USDA SR Legacy fried chicken and french fries entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Jollibee Chicken Loaded Fries with Sriracha Sauce exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: (1) French fries, fast food (FDC ID 172934) contributing approximately 55% of dish weight; (2) Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, fried (FDC ID 171477) contributing approximately 30% of dish weight; (3) Sriracha-style hot sauce and cheese sauce contributing approximately 15% of dish weight, estimated from USDA condiment entries. All per-100g values are derived from this weighted blend. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — french fries and fried potato products
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Chicken Loaded Fries with Sriracha Sauce. GI of 63 is adopted from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for deep-fried french fries (GI approximately 63, medium category), which represents the dominant carbohydrate component of this dish. The chicken and sriracha sauce components contribute negligible additional glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 26.5 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 46.2 g GL for a 350 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1470 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single 350 g serving provides approximately 1470 mg sodium, representing over 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive adults.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic load and refined carbohydrate content from deep-fried potato fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this dish as the estimated glycemic load of approximately 46 g per serving from deep-fried fries can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and pairing with low-GI foods are strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium from deep-frying and processed sauces
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is high in saturated fat (~3.2 g per 100 g), contains measurable trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g), and is very high in sodium, all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; regular consumption is not recommended for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Loaded Fries with Sriracha Sauce is a fast-food composite dish served at Jollibee Singapore, consisting of deep-fried potato fries topped with pieces of fried chicken, a spicy sriracha-flavoured sauce, cheese sauce, and spring onions. The dish is part of the Jollibee Singapore menu and is classified as a meal option (Meal D). The fries are crispy and golden, the chicken pieces are seasoned and fried, and the sriracha sauce adds a tangy, moderately spicy heat profile. The overall flavour is savoury, spicy, and rich, with a combination of crunchy and soft textures. Nutritionally, this is an energy-dense, ultra-processed fast-food item providing approximately 248 kcal per 100 g, with significant contributions from carbohydrates (26.5 g), fat (12.4 g), and protein (9.8 g). The sodium content is high at approximately 420 mg per 100 g, making a full serving a significant source of dietary sodium. The dish is halal-certified as served at Jollibee Singapore. Clinically, this dish presents concerns for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, elevated glycemic load, saturated fat, and trans fat content. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. The dish is a popular fast-food indulgence in Singapore and is representative of the loaded fries trend in Southeast Asian quick-service restaurants.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Jollibee Chicken Loaded Fries with Sriracha Sauce exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are derived from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for deep-fried french fries, fried chicken breast, and condiment components. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for deep-fried french fries as the dominant carbohydrate source. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component sources used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this composite estimate. All three -1 values reflect genuine absence of data in the approved source materials, not assumed zero values. Nutrient values are composite estimates; a direct laboratory analysis or official branded nutrition panel from Jollibee Singapore would improve confidence to high. The dish is tagged as Halal based on Jollibee Singapore's halal certification status as listed on field survey data. Trans fat value of 0.2 g per 100 g is estimated from USDA data for deep-fried fast-food potato products and may vary by frying oil used. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate for a standard loaded fries meal portion; actual weight may vary.
